title: HUMANIN
description: Humanin is a 24-amino acid mitochondrial-derived peptide (MDP) that acts as a potent cytoprotective and neuroprotective factor. It works by inhibiting apoptosis through interactions with Bax and Bcl-2 proteins and by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation across multiple organ systems.

research.title: Humanin Prevents Age-Related Cognitive Decline in Mice and is Associated with Improved Cognitive Age in Humans
research.summary: This study found that humanin administration improves memory in aged mice and that higher naturally occurring levels in humans are associated with better cognitive aging.

research.title: The mitochondrial derived peptide humanin is a regulator of lifespan and healthspan
research.summary: Researchers demonstrated that humanin overexpression increases lifespan in C. elegans and improves metabolic health and inflammatory markers in middle-aged mice.

research.title: Humanin and Its Pathophysiological Roles in Aging: A Systematic Review
research.summary: A comprehensive review highlighting humanin's role in protecting against neurodegeneration, cardiovascular disease, and metabolic disorders like diabetes.

research.title: Protective Mechanism of Humanin Against Oxidative Stress in Aging-Related Cardiovascular Diseases
research.summary: This research details how humanin protects the heart and blood vessels by reducing oxidative stress and maintaining mitochondrial function.

sentiment.summary: Users and researchers alike view Humanin as a highly promising anti-aging and neuroprotective agent with few reported side effects and strong theoretical backing.

effects.label: Cognition:
effects.description: Highly effective at preserving memory and learning capabilities in aging populations

effects.label: Longevity:
effects.description: Strongly correlated with healthspan extension and preserved mitochondrial function

effects.label: Cardiovascular:
effects.description: Shows significant promise in protecting vascular endothelium and preventing atherosclerosis

effects.label: Animal Models:
effects.description: Exceptional results in rodents, though human clinical trial data for direct supplementation is still emerging

dosage.label: HNG Analog:
dosage.description: The potent derivative HNG is often preferred in research settings due to its 1000-fold higher activity than natural Humanin

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Research protocols often utilize twice-weekly administration for healthspan maintenance

dosage.label: Administration:
dosage.description: Typically administered via subcutaneous injection in experimental settings for optimal bioavailability

dosage.label: Biological Markers:
dosage.description: Circulating levels naturally decline with age, making 'replacement' levels a target for longevity enthusiasts

sideEffects.label: Minimal Risks:
sideEffects.description: Humanin is an endogenous peptide, and studies generally report an excellent safety profile with few side effects

sideEffects.label: Injection Site Irritation:
sideEffects.description: As with most peptide injections, localized redness or soreness may occur

sideEffects.label: Potential Growth Interaction:
sideEffects.description: High levels are found in centenarians, but its interaction with growth hormone pathways requires careful monitoring

sideEffects.label: Theoretical Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Long-term effects of chronic supra-physiological levels in humans remain largely unstudied

availability.label: Research Chemicals:
availability.description: Primarily available through peptide supply companies for laboratory and research use

availability.label: Endogenous Boosting:
availability.description: Lifestyle factors like exercise and cold exposure are sometimes discussed as natural ways to support Humanin levels

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Not currently FDA-approved for human medical treatment, strictly classified as a research compound
